description: A single huge backfill can lock tables or time out.
body:
Use bounded batches with progress and restartability.

Use only as a diagnostic do-not-use signal; do not expose this as positive strategy, methodology, or memory. Use when a proposed approach skips schema deltas, query shape, transaction ownership, constraints, locks, and data volume or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in migration dry-runs, rollback checks, EXPLAIN output, constraint tests, and fixture data comparisons. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause data loss, long locks, non-rollbackable writes, silent query-plan regressions, and unsafe backfills.
